articles.title: A Fault Line in Full Bloom
articles.summary: Plains around the San Andreas Fault and across Carrizo Plain National Monument are awash with yellow as wildflowers bloom.

articles.title: Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass
articles.summary:
Swissto12, the Switzerland-based small geostationary satellite specialist, has signed a contract to build the first optical relay spacecraft using its washing machine-sized HummingSat platform.
The post Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: SpaceX offers details on orbital data center satellites
articles.summary:
SpaceX Chief Executive Elon Musk revealed more technical, but not financial, details about his company’s plans to deploy an orbital data center constellation.
The post SpaceX offers details on orbital data center satellites appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 29 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral
articles.summary: Liftoff of the Starlink 10-62 mission from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 10:47 a.m. EDT (1447 UTC). The rocket will fly on a northeasterly trajectory.

articles.title: ESA Regains Contact With Lost Proba-3 Spacecraft
articles.summary:
The European Space Agency has regained contact with the Coronagraph spacecraft of its Proba-3 mission after more than a month of silence. Launched aboard an ISRO PSLV-XL rocket in December 2024, the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph and Occulter spacecraft work in tandem to create and observe an artificial solar eclipse that enables observations of the Sun’s […]
The post ESA Regains Contact With Lost Proba-3 Spacecraft appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Some European Launcher Challenge funding remains in limbo
articles.summary:
Nearly 140 million euros ($162 million) that European Space Agency member states allocated to a program to support launch vehicle development remains in limbo and could be lost.
The post Some European Launcher Challenge funding remains in limbo appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis 2 returns to the pad for April launch attempt
articles.summary:
The Artemis 2 launch vehicle and spacecraft have returned to the launch pad for a launch as soon as April 1.
